named.c(3) Library Functions Manual named.c(3)

NAME

named.c - Named object registry for debugging.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct named_entry
Registry entry for uthash. struct named_registry_t
Global registry with uthash and rwlock. struct named_iter_entry_t

Macros


#define MAX_NAME_LEN 256
#define DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE 768

Typedefs


typedef struct named_entry named_entry_t
Registry entry for uthash.

Functions


asciichat_error_t named_init (void)
void named_destroy (void)
const char * named_register (uintptr_t key, const char *base_name, const char *type, const char *format_spec, const char *file, int line, const char *func, uintptr_t parent_key)
const char * named_register_fmt (uintptr_t key, const char *type, const char *format_spec, const char *file, int line, const char *func, const char *fmt,...)
void named_unregister (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_update_name (uintptr_t key, const char *new_base_name)
const char * named_get (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_get_type (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_get_format_spec (uintptr_t key)
const char * named_describe (uintptr_t key, const char *type_hint)
const char * named_describe_thread (void *thread)
void named_registry_for_each (named_iter_callback_t callback, void *user_data)
const char * named_search_by_type_id (const char *type, void *id)
const char * named_register_fd (int fd, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
const char * named_get_fd (int fd)
const char * named_get_fd_format_spec (int fd)
const char * named_register_packet_type (int pkt_type, const char *file, int line, const char *func)
const char * named_get_packet_type (int pkt_type)
const char * named_get_packet_type_format_spec (int pkt_type)
void named_registry_register_packet_types (void)

Detailed Description

Named object registry for debugging.

Provides a centralized registry for naming any addressable resource. Uses uthash for O(1) lookup by uintptr_t key. Rwlock protects the hash table with minimal critical section.

Definition in file debug/named.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

typedef struct named_entry named_entry_t

Registry entry for uthash.

Function Documentation

const char * named_describe (uintptr_t key, const char * type_hint)

Definition at line 336 of file debug/named.c.

336                                                                  {
337   if (!type_hint) type_hint = "object";
338 
339   static _Thread_local char buffer[DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE];
340 
341   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
342     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint, (ptrdiff_t)key);
343     return buffer;
344   }
345 
346   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
347   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
348   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
349   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
350 
351   if (entry) {
352     const char *type = entry->type ? entry->type : type_hint;
353     const char *name = entry->name ? entry->name : "unknown";
354     if (entry->file && entry->func && entry->line > 0) {
355       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx) @ %s:%d:%s()", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key,
356                entry->file, entry->line, entry->func);
357     } else {
358       snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s/%s (0x%tx)", type, name, (ptrdiff_t)key);
359     }
360   } else {
361     snprintf(buffer, sizeof(buffer), "%s (0x%tx)", type_hint ? type_hint : "unknown", (ptrdiff_t)key);
362   }
363 
364   return buffer;
365 }




References DESCRIBE_BUFFER_SIZE, named_entry::file,
    named_entry::func, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    named_entry::line, named_entry::name, and
    named_entry::type.

const
  char * named_describe_thread (void * thread)


Definition at line 367 of file debug/named.c.


367                                                 {
368   uintptr_t key = asciichat_thread_to_key((asciichat_thread_t)thread);
369   return named_describe(key, "thread");
370 }






References named_describe().






void
  named_destroy (void )


Definition at line 93 of file debug/named.c.


93                          {
94   // Always attempt to shut down the lifecycle, but always cleanup entries
95   lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
96 
97   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
98 
99   // Find the registry's own rwlock entry (allocated during named_init bootstrap)
100   // This entry will be unregistered by rwlock_destroy, so we must not free it here
101   uintptr_t registry_lock_key = (uintptr_t)(const void *)&g_named_registry.entries_lock;
102   named_entry_t *registry_lock_entry = NULL;
103   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &registry_lock_key, sizeof(uintptr_t), registry_lock_entry);
104 
105   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL;) {
106     named_entry_t *next = e->hh.next;
107 
108     // Skip the registry lock entry - it will be cleaned up by rwlock_destroy
109     // which calls NAMED_UNREGISTER after we release the lock
110     if (e == registry_lock_entry) {
111       e = next;
112       continue;
113     }
114 
115     // Free all other entries
116     free(e->name);
117     if (e->type) free(e->type);
118     if (e->format_spec) free(e->format_spec);
119     if (e->file) free(e->file);
120     if (e->func) free(e->func);
121     free(e);
122     e = next;
123   }
124 
125   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
126 
127   // rwlock_destroy will call NAMED_UNREGISTER on the lock entry, which will:
128   // 1. Acquire the lock (now safe since we released it)
129   // 2. Find and remove the lock entry from the hash
130   // 3. Free the lock entry's fields
131   rwlock_destroy(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
132 }






References lifecycle_shutdown().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_destroy().






const
  char * named_get (uintptr_t key)


Definition at line 297 of file debug/named.c.


297                                      {
298   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
299     return NULL;
300   }
301 
302   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
303   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
304   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
305   const char *result = entry ? entry->name : NULL;
306   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
307   return result;
308 }

    named_entry::name.

  char * named_get_fd (int fd)

451   if (fd < 0) return NULL;
452 
453   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
454     return NULL;
455   }
456 
457   uintptr_t key = encode_fd_key(fd);
458   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
459   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
460   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
461   const char *result = entry ? entry->name : NULL;
462   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
463   return result;
464 }

asciichat_error_t
  named_init (void )


Definition at line 74 of file debug/named.c.


74                                    {
75   // Idempotent: if already initialized, return success
76   if (lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
77     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
78   }
79 
80   if (!lifecycle_init(&g_named_registry.lifecycle, "named_registry")) {
81     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
82   }
83 
84   if (rwlock_init(&g_named_registry.entries_lock, "named_registry_lock") != 0) {
85     lifecycle_shutdown(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
86     return ASCIICHAT_OK;  // Continue even if rwlock init fails
87   }
88 
89   lifecycle_init_commit(&g_named_registry.lifecycle);
90   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
91 }






References lifecycle_init(),
    lifecycle_init_commit(), lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    lifecycle_shutdown(), and rwlock_init().


Referenced by asciichat_shared_init(), and
  main().






const
  char * named_register (uintptr_t key, const char * base_name, const char *
  type, const char * format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func, uintptr_t parent_key)


Definition at line 168 of file debug/named.c.


169                                                                                                {
170   if (!base_name || !type || !format_spec) {
171     return "?";
172   }
173 
174   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
175     return base_name;
176   }
177 
178   // If parent_key provided, look up parent name and create hierarchical name
179   char final_base_name[256];
180   if (parent_key != 0) {
181     rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
182     const char *parent_name = named_lookup_name_unlocked(parent_key);
183     rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
184 
185     if (parent_name) {
186       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s#%s", parent_name, base_name);
187     } else {
188       // Parent not found, fall back to base name
189       snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
190     }
191   } else {
192     snprintf(final_base_name, sizeof(final_base_name), "%s", base_name);
193   }
194 
195   // Generate unique name using atomic counters (only for non-hierarchical names)
196   char name_buffer[256];
197   if (parent_key == 0) {
198     uint64_t counter = 0;
199     if (strcmp(type, "mutex") == 0) {
200       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&mutex_counter, 1);
201     } else if (strcmp(type, "rwlock") == 0) {
202       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&rwlock_counter, 1);
203     } else if (strcmp(type, "cond") == 0) {
204       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&cond_counter, 1);
205     } else if (strcmp(type, "atomic") == 0) {
206       counter = __sync_fetch_and_add(&atomic_counter, 1);
207     }
208     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s.%"PRIu64, final_base_name, counter);
209   } else {
210     // Hierarchical names don't get counters - parent already has one
211     snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "%s", final_base_name);
212   }
213 
214   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock (avoid long critical section)
215   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
216   if (!entry) return base_name;
217 
218   entry->key = key;
219   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
220   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
221 
222   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
223   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
224   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
225   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
226 
227   return entry->name;
228 }






References named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






const
  char * named_register_fd (int fd, const char * file, int line, const char *
  func)


Definition at line 421 of file debug/named.c.


421                                                                                     {
422   if (fd < 0) {
423     return "?";
424   }
425 
426   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
427     return "?";
428   }
429 
430   char name_buffer[256];
431   snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "fd=%d", fd);
432 
433   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
434   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
435   if (!entry) return "?";
436 
437   uintptr_t key = encode_fd_key(fd);
438   entry->key = key;
439   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
440   named_entry_init(entry, "fd", "%d", file, line, func);
441 
442   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
443   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
444   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
445   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
446 
447   return entry->name;
448 }






References named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






const
  char * named_register_fmt (uintptr_t key, const char * type, const char *
  format_spec, const char * file, int line, const char * func, const char * fmt,
  ...)


Definition at line 230 of file debug/named.c.


231                                                                        {
232   if (!type || !format_spec || !fmt) {
233     return "?";
234   }
235 
236   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
237     return fmt;
238   }
239 
240   // Format the name BEFORE acquiring lock
241   va_list args;
242   va_start(args, fmt);
243   char *full_name = NULL;
244   int ret = vasprintf(&full_name, fmt, args);
245   va_end(args);
246 
247   if (ret < 0) {
248     return "?";
249   }
250 
251   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
252   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
253   if (!entry) {
254     free(full_name);
255     return "?";
256   }
257 
258   // Initialize entry (name is pre-allocated as full_name)
259   entry->key = key;
260   entry->name = full_name;
261   named_entry_init(entry, type, format_spec, file, line, func);
262 
263   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
264   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
265   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
266   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
267 
268   return entry->name;
269 }






References args, named_entry::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), and named_entry::name.






const
  char * named_register_packet_type (int pkt_type, const char * file, int line,
  const char * func)


Definition at line 482 of file debug/named.c.


482                                                                                                    {
483   if (pkt_type < 0) {
484     return "?";
485   }
486 
487   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
488     return "?";
489   }
490 
491   char name_buffer[256];
492   snprintf(name_buffer, sizeof(name_buffer), "PACKET_TYPE=%d", pkt_type);
493 
494   // Allocate entry BEFORE acquiring lock
495   named_entry_t *entry = malloc(sizeof(named_entry_t));
496   if (!entry) return "?";
497 
498   uintptr_t key = encode_pkt_type_key(pkt_type);
499   entry->key = key;
500   entry->name = entry_strdup(name_buffer);
501   named_entry_init(entry, "packet_type", "%d", file, line, func);
502 
503   // Only hold lock for the hash table operation
504   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
505   HASH_ADD(hh, g_named_registry.entries, key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
506   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
507 
508   return entry->name;
509 }






References named_entry::key,

void
  named_registry_for_each (named_iter_callback_t callback, void *
  user_data)


Definition at line 377 of file debug/named.c.


377                                                                               {
378   if (!callback || !lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
379     return;
380   }
381 
382   // Snapshot entries while holding read lock
383   named_iter_entry_t entries[256];
384   int count = 0;
385 
386   rwlock_rdlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
387   for (named_entry_t *e = g_named_registry.entries; e != NULL && count < 256; e = e->hh.next) {
388     entries[count].key = e->key;
389     strncpy(entries[count].name, e->name ? e->name : "?", MAX_NAME_LEN - 1);
390     entries[count].name[MAX_NAME_LEN - 1] = '\0';
391     count++;
392   }
393   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
394 
395   // Call callback outside the lock
396   for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
397     callback(entries[i].key, entries[i].name, user_data);
398   }
399 }






References named_iter_entry_t::key,
    lifecycle_is_initialized(), MAX_NAME_LEN, and
    named_iter_entry_t::name.

void
  named_unregister (uintptr_t key)


Definition at line 271 of file debug/named.c.


271                                      {
272   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_named_registry.lifecycle)) {
273     return;
274   }
275 
276   rwlock_wrlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
277   named_entry_t *entry = NULL;
278   HASH_FIND(hh, g_named_registry.entries, &key, sizeof(uintptr_t), entry);
279   if (entry) {
280     HASH_DEL(g_named_registry.entries, entry);
281     free(entry->name);
282     if (entry->type) free(entry->type);
283     if (entry->format_spec) free(entry->format_spec);
284     if (entry->file) free(entry->file);
285     if (entry->func) free(entry->func);
286     free(entry);
287   }
288   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_named_registry.entries_lock);
289 }

    named_entry::format_spec, named_entry::func,
